name: GridLayout2

unions.name: GridMargin
unions.comment:
An option for grid margins.

  - `SameAsGutter` – the minimal modular grid margin will be the same as the gutter.
  - `GridMargin` – allows to specify the minimal modular grid margin manually.

unions.name: ScreenClass
unions.comment:
A screen class. Similar to `Element.DeviceClass` from `elm-ui`,
but narrowed down to support only 2 devices both in grid layout and in the application code.
Names differe from `Element.DeviceClass` to avoid import conflicts
when importing everything from both `Element` and `GridLayout2`.

aliases.name: LayoutConfig
aliases.comment:
Layout configuration. Needs to be passed in the `init` function once per app.

  - `mobileScreen.minGridWidth` – Includes grid margins.
    The MobileScreen Figma layouts should use this width first.
    If window width is less than this value, we display horizontal scroll.
  - `mobileScreen.maxGridWidth` – Includes grid margins.
    The MobileScreen Figma layouts can use this width for an additional example.
    If not set, then the grid will stretch util the next screen breakpoint.
  - `desktopScreen.minGridWidth` – Includes grid margins.
    The DesktopScreen Figma layouts should use this width first.
    If window width is equal or greater than this value, the screen class is DesktopScreen.
  - `desktopScreen.maxGridWidth` – Includes grid margins.
    The DesktopScreen Figma layouts can use this width for an additional example.
    If not set, then the grid will stretch indefinitely.
  - `columnCount` – The number of columns in the grid.
  - `gutter` – The width of the gutter between columns, in pixels.
  - `margin` – The minimal modular grid margin. Can be `SameAsGutter` or `GridMargin` with specific value, in pixels.
aliases.type: { mobileScreen : { minGridWidth : Basics.Int, maxGridWidth : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, columnCount : Basics.Int, gutter : Basics.Int, margin : GridLayout2.GridMargin }, desktopScreen : { minGridWidth : Basics.Int, maxGridWidth : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, columnCount : Basics.Int, gutter : Basics.Int, margin : GridLayout2.GridMargin } }

aliases.name: LayoutState
aliases.comment:
Layout state. A value of this type contains everything needed to render a layout or any grid-aware element.

  - `window` – the current window size.
  - `screenClass` – the current screen class.
  - `config` – the layout configuration. Not to be changed during the app lifecycle, or even accessed directly.
  - `grid` – the current grid settings, calculated based on the window size and the screen class. Use them anywhere in the app.
aliases.type: { window : GridLayout2.WindowSize, screenClass : GridLayout2.ScreenClass, config : GridLayout2.LayoutConfig, grid : { contentWidth : Basics.Int, columnCount : Basics.Int, gutter : Basics.Int, margin : Basics.Int } }

aliases.name: WindowSize
aliases.comment: A window size object coming from Flags, and cunstructed from the `Browser.Events.onResize` event.

values.name: bodyAttributes
values.comment: A helper to build the application `Layout`. See Readme for example usage.

values.name: gridBox
values.comment:
A helper to be used in application pages.
Sets both container width and height in terms of modular grid steps,
which allows the element design to have not only predictable width, but also predictable height.
See Readme for example usage.
values.type: GridLayout2.LayoutState -> { widthSteps : Basics.Int, heightSteps : Basics.Int } -> List.List (Element.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Element.Element msg) -> Element.Element msg

values.name: gridColumn
values.comment:
A helper to be used in application pages.
Sets container width in terms of modular grid steps, and allows arbitrary height.
See Readme for example usage.
values.type: GridLayout2.LayoutState -> { widthSteps : Basics.Int } -> List.List (Element.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Element.Element msg) -> Element.Element msg

values.name: gridRow
values.comment: A helper to be used in application pages. See Readme for example usage.

values.name: heightOfGridSteps
values.comment: A helper to be used in application pages with `elm-ui` whenever `gridColumn` and `gridBox` don't math your needs.

values.name: init
values.comment: Initializes the layout state, which then needs to be stored in some sort of `Shared.Model`.

values.name: layoutInnerAttributes
values.comment: A helper to build the application `Layout`. See Readme for example usage.

values.name: layoutOuterAttributes
values.comment: A helper to build the application `Layout`. See Readme for example usage.

values.name: scaleProportionallyToWidthOfGridSteps
values.comment: A to scale an element to specified width of steps, maintaining the original proportions (e.g. of an image which you want never to be cropped).
values.type: GridLayout2.LayoutState -> { originalWidth : Basics.Int, originalHeight : Basics.Int, widthSteps : Basics.Int } -> List.List (Element.Attribute msg)

values.name: scaleProportionallyToWidthOfGridStepsFloat
values.comment:
An implementation detail, but can be used directly in applications without `elm-ui`.
Returns the width and height of block scaled to width of specified number of grid steps (including gutters), in pixels, Float.
values.type: GridLayout2.LayoutState -> { originalWidth : Basics.Int, originalHeight : Basics.Int, widthSteps : Basics.Int } -> { width : Basics.Float, height : Basics.Float }

values.name: update
values.comment: Updates the layout state. The previous state is passed from the `Shared.Model`.

values.name: widthOfGridSteps
values.comment: A helper to be used in application pages with `elm-ui` whenever `gridColumn` and `gridBox` don't math your needs.

values.name: widthOfGridStepsFloat
values.comment:
An implementation detail, but can be used directly in applications without `elm-ui`.
Returns the width of specified number of grid steps (including gutters), in pixels, Float.

values.name: windowSizeDecoder
values.comment: A decoder for the `WindowSize` type, for Flags.
